channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: ICE arrests 16 illegal migrants caught fleeing out back of Mississippi business during raid
item.description: A Mississippi worksite raid by federal immigration agents resulted in the arrests of 16 illegal immigrants, according to an ICE news release.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New details released in Lake Oswego shooting that damaged SAIF CEO's home
item.description: Authorities are seeking information about a suspect believed to have fired multiple rounds into the home of the CEO of an Oregon workers' compensation insurance organization, causing damage to the front door and minor damage to the home.
item.dc:creator: Aimee Plante, NewsNation

item.title: OPM outlines stricter accountability for executive employees
item.description: The acting OPM director issued the memo to agency heads after President Donald Trump signed an executive order.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Texas lottery drawings that paid out big jackpots are the focus of widening investigatons
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — Two major lottery drawings in Texas that put nearly $180 million in the pockets of winning ticket holders have set off widening state investigations over concern that ticket sellers and buyers may have exploited the rules. The Texas Lottery, one of the largest in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: NADIA LATHAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: DOGE says Texas nonprofit with former Biden transition member reaped millions operating empty facility
item.description: Texas nonprofit reaped millions of dollars operating an empty Texas housing facility, the Department of Government Efficient said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Justice Department abandoning cases alleging discriminatory police and firefighter hiring
item.description: The Justice Department is abandoning Biden administration cases that sought to force police and fire departments to end alleged discriminatory hiring practices, Attorney General Pam Bondi said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Alanna Durkin Richer, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Police killings set record in 2024, but with smallest increase in years: Report
item.description: In 2024, police officers in the US killed 1,365 people, a slight increase from the 1,329 civilians killed in 2023, according to the Mapping Police Violence report.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, Fox 5

item.title: Bell tolls as New York marks the anniversary of the 1993 World Trade Center bombing
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York City on Wednesday marked the anniversary of the 1993 bombing at the old World Trade Center, when Islamic extremists first attempted to bring down the twin towers with 1,200 pounds of explosives in a parked rental van.
item.dc:creator: JOHN MINCHILLO, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: 3 councilmen in South Carolina's third-largest city charged in bribery and kickback scheme
item.description: Three members of the City Council in South Carolina's third-largest city accepted kickbacks and bribes to give out grant money and rezone land or lied about what they knew, federal prosecutors said Wednesday, Charges against North Charleston councilmen Jerome Heyward, Sandino Moses and Mike A.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Judge criticizes Justice Department's broad reading of Trump's Capitol riot pardons
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge on Wednesday criticized the Justice Department's evolving position that a presidential pardon for a Kentucky man who stormed the Capitol also covers his conviction for illegally possessing guns at his home. U.S.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Kroger giving out free boxes for Daylight Savings Time
item.description: FARMERSBURG, Ind. (WTWO/WAWV)— The time change is less than two weeks away and Kroger is hoping to make that change a little easier. Starting on March 4 at noon, Kroger will be giving away what they refer to as the Hour Back Box.
item.dc:creator: Christopher Boyll, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Princeton 911 call about fire, dead body at luxury complex led to discovery of slain star athlete brother
item.description: The Princeton, New Jersey, man accused of killing his brother also reportedly set the family cat on fire in an apartment not far from Princeton University.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Search underway for Colorado man in Black Canyon of the Gunnison National Park
item.description: Officials are searching for Jordan Marsters, a 31-year-old who disappeared two weeks ago, in Black Canyon of the Gunnison National Park in Colorado.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Michelle Trachtenberg, 'Gossip Girl' and 'Buffy' actress, dead at 39
item.description: Actress Michelle Trachtenberg has died, police sources confirmed to multiple news outlets on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, NewsNation

item.title: Supreme Court seems likely to rule for Ohio woman claiming job bias because she's straight
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court seemed likely Wednesday to side with an Ohio woman who claims she suffered sex discrimination from her employer because she is straight.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NFLPA report cards: Dolphins, Vikings finish at the top for second straight year
item.description: The Miami Dolphins are the top-ranked team, followed by the Minnesota Vikings, for the second consecutive season in the NFL Players Association report card.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Navy widow's long wait for burial at sea about to end
item.description: Wanda Davis never dreamed it would take her 14 months to say the words "now we can have some closure and move on.
item.dc:creator: Chris Horne, NewsNation

item.title: The US Christian population has declined for years. A new survey shows that drop leveling off
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The number of Americans who identify as Christian has declined steadily for years, but that drop shows signs of slowing, according to a new survey Wednesday from the Pew Research Center. The Religious Landscape Study finds 62% of U.S. adults call themselves Christians.
item.dc:creator: TIFFANY STANLEY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: American Airlines flight discontinues landing to avoid departing plane in Virginia
item.description: The maneuver occurred less than two hours before another plane attempting to land at Chicago’s Midway Airport was forced to climb back into the sky to avoid another aircraft crossing the runway.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Trump pitches 'gold card' as replacement for EB5 investor visa
item.description: The Trump administration will be revamping the U.S. investor visa, increasing the funds required to secure the status while dubbing the new option a "gold card.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, WGN 9

item.title: RFK Jr. targets childhood psychiatric drugs; doctors push back
item.description: Should Kennedy seek to limit access to these medications, there are some avenues available to him in his new role.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, NewsNation

item.title: Mexican drug cartel leader extradited to the US to face federal drug trafficking charges
item.description: Federal authorities extradited Mexican cartel leader Jesus Ricardo Patron Sanchez to the United States to face federal narcotics trafficking charges.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Adnan Syed's murder conviction still stands as he seeks sentence reduction in 'Serial' case
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — Despite documented problems with the evidence against him and an earlier request from prosecutors to clear his record, Adnan Syed will remain convicted of murder, according to court papers filed Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: LEA SKENE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: University suspends fraternity accused of ‘inhumane’ hazing that left pledge in fear
item.description: A fraternity at Miami University in Ohio has been suspended after a student complained of "inhumane" hazing and undergoing abuse, according to a hazing incident report.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: RFK Jr. targets childhood psychiatric drugs; doctors push back
item.description: Childhood psychiatrists insist the drugs, for attention-deficit/hyperactivity disorder (ADHD) and depression, are nonaddictive and proven safe and say they are more concerned about young Americans unable to access psychiatric medications that could help.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, KRON4

item.title: WATCH: Officers cut open man's 'narco wig' at Colombian airport to reveal cocaine
item.description: He may have been using his head, but a 40-year-old passenger wasn't able to get past Colombian airport security with a cocaine-stuffed "narco wig," police said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, Fox 5

item.title: Judge OKs prison abuse settlement, rejecting Trump administration's push to rewrite protections
item.description: A judge on Tuesday gave final approval to a legal settlement that subjects the federal Bureau of Prisons to independent monitoring and increased transparency in the wake of rampant staff-on-inmate sexual abuse at a now-shuttered federal women’s prison in California. U.S.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Woman injured on Harry Potter theme park ride awarded $7.25 million
item.description: A federal jury has awarded $7.25 million to a 74-year-old Arizona woman for a spine injury she suffered in a fall while exiting the Harry Potter and the Forbidden Journey ride at Universal Studios Hollywood.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Judge indefinitely blocks Trump’s plan to freeze federal aid
item.description: A federal judge on Tuesday indefinitely blocked the Trump administration’s freeze of federal funding, dealing a stark blow to President Trump’s sweeping efforts to realign government spending with his agenda. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WGN 9

item.title: What to know about the Feb. 28 Economic Blackout
item.description: Customers across the United States are looking to send a message Friday to companies that have rolled back their DEI initiatives in the form of a 24-hour consumer boycott, also known as the “Feb. 28 Economic Blackout.
item.dc:creator: Daesha Gear, KRON4

item.title: WATCH: Man caught hiding $10K of cocaine in toupée
item.description: A man was arrested in Columbia after trying to board a flight while wearing a wig hiding cocaine worth more than $10,000.
item.dc:creator: Celeste Houmard, WFLA, NBC

item.title: FCC chairman climbs Nexstar broadcast tower to promote industry, jobs
item.description: The nation's top communications regulator made a stop in Baldwin County Monday morning and you might be surprised why.
item.dc:creator: Debbie Williams, Fox 5

item.title: Supreme Court orders new trial for Oklahoma death row inmate
item.description: The Supreme Court on Tuesday sided with an Oklahoma death row inmate who claimed alongside the state itself that his trial was unfair, ordering a new trial and staving off his execution.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WGN 9

item.title: What will win at a nailbiter Oscars? AP's film writers make their predictions
item.description: By the time the Oscars wind around after months of other award shows and constant handicapping, the prizes can feel almost predetermined. Not this year.
item.dc:creator: JAKE COYLE and LINDSEY BAHR, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Why you won't receive a Supplemental Security Income check in March
item.description: It's the latest blip in the payment schedule but don't worry — beneficiaries will still receive all of their payments.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KRON4

item.title: Musk extends deadline to describe work
item.description: More than two million federal workers remain uncertain about their job security after last night's deadline to respond to an email from Elon Musk, the man President Donald Trump has tapped to make the government more efficient.
item.dc:creator: Reshad Hudson, WGN 9

item.title: Student loan borrowers face abrupt 180 as GOP budget plans threaten to raise payments
item.description: Student loan borrowers could see their payments increase under budget plans currently under discussion by House Republicans, a violent about-face after years of working with the Biden administration to lower payments and secure debt relief.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, WFLA, NBC
